metadata.Do you have any of the following as a re: Digital Gadgets are used till right before bed
metadata.Can you describe the problems?: Insomnia, Bad Dreams, Trouble Falling Asleep, Interrupted Sleep
metadata.Which of these challenges do you face of: Difficulty in Learning/Understanding/Focusing on a new Concept
metadata.How would you describe your learning pat: I don't learn as quickly as some people, but I have excellent retention and a long memory.
metadata.How will you describe your body frame?: Slim, light frame, tall or short, bony
metadata.How would you best describe your body te: I sweat easily and can feel damp or moist to touch
metadata.How would you describe your metabolism?: Fast metabolism, difficult to gain weight
metadata.How would you describe your appetite?: Erratic - I am an irregular eater with fluctuating appetite and habits
metadata.How would you describe your faeces (stoo: Moderate, solid, pale, mucous in the stool (if constipated stools are still soft)

tokens.#TID020: Having a Prevotella-rich gut microbiome possibly indicates weight loss, decreases cholesterol levels, and limits the bifidogenic effect in individuals consuming a fibre-rich diet. When in high abundance though, Prevotella is known to cause sleep disturbances. These can make your circadian rhythm(sleep cycle) erratic possibly leaving you tired during the day.
tokens.#TID024: Supposed to have a probiotic effect on the body, Blautia contributes to regulating health by reducing metabolic disorders. Blautia is being decreased to enhance your sleep efficiency and duration while also alleviating the low phases.
tokens.#TID028: An interesting bacteria commonly seen in the human gut, Collinsella is often a marker for the metabolic condition of the body. The bacteria ferments plant and animal origin carbs to produce lactate that converts to energy. Our feeling of wellness is impacted by our gut and the gut can bring our overall health levels up. To retain its diversity and equilibrium, the Iom optimizer is bringing this bacteria down.
tokens.#TID032: A very common gut bacteria, Fusicatenibacter is a happy sugar fermenter with anti inflammatory properties. And in the process of this fermentation they produce the good SCFAs-butyrate and propionate for you. However, this bacteria might be pulling your gut away from peaceful sleep. Iom recommends reducing this bacteria to enhance your sleep quality
tokens.#TID036: Coprococcus is a normal resident of your gut flora. These bacteria have anti-inflammatory and anti-bacterial properties. They also work at strengthening the intestinal gut barrier.You might wonder at times why your stress and sleep quality arent as manageable as you would like. Coprococcus abundance negatively impacts both stress and sleep. And Iom optimiser suggests for better SEnS, reduce this one.
tokens.#TID040: A good bacteria with a prebiotic impact on your gut, Faecalibacterium is an important fella that produces butyrate, and propionate, maintains gut equilibrium, and good gut health. Along with enabling the energy production process, Faecalibacterium are great sleep enhancers. Increasing this one to better your sleep quality and lower any anxiety you deal with.
tokens.#TID044: Bacteroides play multiple roles in the gut and keep the system healthy. Contributing to the immune system, they enable expelling toxins and fighting pathogens. These fairly common resident of the gut are being increased for you to ensure better gut health and a movement towards elevated SEnS.
tokens.#TID048: These probiotic, immunity enhancing bacteria live in your intestines and help body fight diarrhea, constipation, and other intestinal disorders. Your performance is defined by your SEnS. Increase your Bifidobacterium to ensure that every game is a win. This bacteria helps your battle with anxiety as well as pushes sleep quality and heightens energy levels.
tokens.#TID052: Dialister is often seen associated with gut imbalance and inflammation. The end products of this fella are usually seen in conditions not best.However, for the SEnS compass, this bacteria adds on to your energy levels and make you more vivacious during the day. The Iom optimiser is marginally increasing this to uplift your vitality.
tokens.#TID056: Anaerostipes -an-earo-stipes are those friends in us who break down plant and fruit sugars and Vitamin B into propionate and acetate-both beneficial to your health. The bacteria aids better balancing glucose at fasting levels.The focus on maximising your every day with appropriate energy levels and keeping depression away can be helped by increasing the levels of Anaerostipes
tokens.#TID059: Vata dosha points towards heightened energy levels, light sleep and possibly being prone to worry and anxiety. It might be difficult to follow a routine causing erratic schedules. When at your best, you are creative, mentally fast.
tokens.#TID061: Those with Kapha Dosha generally fall asleep easily and for medium duration. This might result in daytime sleepiness and low energy levels. Stress manifests in Kapha people as increased sluggishness and disenchantment.
tokens.#TID090: Gut diversity is the range of different bacteria living in your gut, showing how balanced and healthy your digestive ecosystem is.High gut diversity means your microbiome has many different bacterial species. This supports better digestion, stronger immunity, efficient nutrient absorption, and resistance to infections.
tokens.#TID094: Dietary fibres, solube and insoluble, are not broken down by human enzymes but actually passed down to the colon where the residing bacteria work on them. These bacteria thereby produce multiple metabolites(chemicals) that in turn affect gut balance, gut barrier, impacting overall health, immunity, resistence to cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular disease and more. However, excessive fibre fermentation/intake is seen with digestive distress ranging from constipation to diarrhoea, bloating, reduction in appetite and early satiety
tokens.#TID098: Certain bacteria are responsible for breaking down complex carbohydrates, fibers, and other substrates into short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs) and other metabolites that serve as energy sources for both the host and other microbial populations. Having a good abundance of energy producing bacteria results in more stable, consistent energy levels through the day, keeeping you bouncing and ready for life.
tokens.#TID102: The interplay between the microbiome and your immune system includes multiple interactions in a state of balance and disease. Microbial signals ensure strong intestinal barrier function, maintain gut balance and elicit a protective immune response during an infection. A Moderate to High abundance of bacteria that enable better immunity provide protection to the body against inflammation and resistance to disease.
tokens.#TID124: Insulin resistance is primarily the body"s reduced response to insulin and is unable to take up glucose from the blood. The microbiome plays a crucial role, contributing to the patho-genesis of obesity and pre-diabetes. A high or moderate abundance of insulin resistance bacteria shows a distinct pattern to carbohydrate metabolism and an increased movement towards metabolic challenges.
tokens.#TID128: The gut microbiota influences food intake by regulating brain function in a number of ways, such as by contributing to the production of chemicals which plays an important role in regulating gastrointestinal function, appetite, regulate fat storage, energy harvest, inflammation & circadian rhythms. While these bacteria also produce SCFAs which are known to release energy, and manage gut balance, they might hamper other efforts towards better health.
tokens.#TID132: Probiotic bacteria exert their beneficial effects through either inhibiting growth of pathogens, or production of helpful metabolites, reinforcement of gut barrier or positively modulating the immune system A moderate to high abundance of these bacteria contributes to various health benefits in particular realted to gut function , digestive health and reducing inflammation.
tokens.#TID136: Protein degraders enable the breakdown of protein from animal and plant sources. They play crucial roles in the amino acid balance and gut function. On average, 6_x0096_18 g of protein reaches the large intestine per day that is roughly proportional to total protein intake . A Moderate abundance of protein metabolising bacteria indicates a fair contribution from your microbiome to your health.
tokens.#TID140: Many complex carbohydrates, sugars and fibre are degraded and fermented by the bacteria in the small and large intestine to produce energy as well as impact health through the metabolites produced. These functions are primarily carried out by the bacteria as we lack the enzymes needed for this function. Carbohydrate-fermenting bacteria contribute to gut health and may have beneficial effects on metabolic health, immune function, and protection against aging and neurocognitive diseases.
tokens.#TID144: Lactic acid bacteria (LAB) are microorganisms that can ferment carbohydrates to produce lactic acid. These bacteria produce myriad beneficial effects for human beings include, alleviation of lactose intolerance, diarrhea, peptic ulcer, stimulation of immune system, lowering cholesterol, and have antiallergic effects, and antifungal actions. A Low abundance of these beneficial bacteria limits your body"s capability to leverage the microbiome towards best health conditions.
tokens.#TID148: Your gut microbiome has a critical role in protecting you from infections from pathogens from outside or the overgrowth of pathogens inside. They are capable of being the resistence of the body to bacteria centric infections or to the possibility of the pathogens (disease causing microbes) overtaking the body. A normal human gut does house some levels of pathogens which may or may not cause any infection or inflammation. Presence of these pathogens does not necessarily imply disease. Your gut has a low abundance of pathogens.
tokens.#TID152: The Firmicutes to Bacteroidetes (F/B) ratio shows the balance between two major groups of gut bacteria that help regulate digestion and metabolism.A high Firmicutes to Bacteroidetes (F/B) ratio means Firmicutes dominate.This may increase calorie extraction from food, contributing to weight gain and metabolic strain.
